commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a macOS launchd install guard that refuses to write/bootstrap a user LaunchAgent when a same-label system LaunchDaemon is loaded or present on disk, with mocked launchd regression tests.

PR surface: Source +48, Tests +75. Total +123 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level with live reporter evidence. Current main and v2026.6.10 still write and bootstrap only the gui-domain LaunchAgent, while the linked issue shows the system LaunchDaemon plus user install path; I did not run a live macOS mutation in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The next action is contributor or maintainer validation: add real macOS proof and have maintainers accept the fail-closed install behavior; no automated code repair is indicated.

target.title: fix(daemon): refuse duplicate launchd gateway managers [AI-assisted]
target.body:
Closes #97178

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where operators running the gateway under a system-domain LaunchDaemon could later run `openclaw gateway install` from a user session and silently add a duplicate gui-domain LaunchAgent for the same gateway label and port. That duplicate manager setup can restart-loop the gateway and disconnect clients.

## Why This Change Was Made

The macOS launchd installer now checks for a same-label system LaunchDaemon before it writes or bootstraps the user LaunchAgent. If `launchctl print system/<label>` succeeds or a matching `/Library/LaunchDaemons/<label>.plist` exists, install refuses with recovery guidance instead of creating a second KeepAlive manager. This intentionally stays narrow and does not add full system LaunchDaemon install/stop/uninstall lifecycle support.

AI-assisted: yes. I understand the change: it adds a pre-write launchd conflict guard and focused mocked launchd regressions for the reported duplicate-manager path.

## User Impact

Headless macOS operators who already manage the gateway as a system LaunchDaemon get a clear stop-and-fix error instead of a second user LaunchAgent that can fight the system daemon on the same port.

## Evidence

Behavior addressed: `openclaw gateway install` refuses to write/bootstrap a gui-domain LaunchAgent when a same-label system LaunchDaemon is loaded or present on disk.

Real environment tested: local source checkout with mocked launchd states; the issue reporter supplied live headless macOS LaunchDaemon evidence on #97178. I did not mutate a real system LaunchDaemon on this host.

Exact steps:

```shell
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/launchd.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/launchd.test.ts src/daemon/inspect.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix: the new launchd tests cover both `launchctl print system/ai.openclaw.gateway` reporting a loaded daemon and `/Library/LaunchDaemons/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist` existing when the system job is not printed.

Observed result: focused launchd test passed; the broader daemon/status proof set passed in 2 Vitest shards.

What was not tested: live install against a disposable headless macOS host with a real system LaunchDaemon, to avoid creating or disturbing host-level launchd services from this automation run.
target.author: harjothkhara
